mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations. A month-over-month drop in FEDFUNDS signals monetary accommodation that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.3% as of 2018-01-15 reflects recent rate cuts from late 2017
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which translates into higher corporate earnings and equity valuations. A sustained 3-month increase in PAYEMS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147,380 thousand as of 2018-01-15 reflects strong labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer spending strength and aggregate demand. When RSAFS accelerates month-over-month, it signals robust economic activity that supports equity earnings, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic output and capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, it signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion when supply constraints tighten
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending. When both HOUST and RSAFS accelerate simultaneously, it indicates broad-based demand strength that drives positive equity returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand-side momentum that single-series signals miss.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,297 thousand and RSAFS at 495,381 million as of 2018-01-15
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS remains low, real rates compress, supporting equity valuations. This interaction captures the regime where monetary policy lags inflation, creating a favorable equity environment over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 247.963 and FEDFUNDS at 1.3% as of 2018-01-15
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power. This threshold-triggered signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline due to lower aggregate demand.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2018-01-15, below trigger threshold
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 53% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly reflects aggregate demand strength. When GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized, it signals late-cycle weakness that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice terminal growth rates downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
